title: Music Theory Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of scales, chords, rhythm, and music notation.
subject: Arts

questions.prompt: How many semitones are in an octave?
questions.explanation: An octave consists of 12 semitones in Western music (the chromatic scale).

questions.prompt: Which note is a perfect fifth above C?
questions.explanation: A perfect fifth above C is G. Counting 7 semitones up from C: C–C#–D–D#–E–F–F#–G.
Forte (f) is an Italian musical term meaning loud or strong.

questions.prompt: A major chord is composed of which intervals?
questions.explanation: A major chord is built from a root, a major third (4 semitones up), and a perfect fifth (7 semitones up).

questions.prompt: What time signature has 3 beats per measure, each beat being a quarter note?
questions.explanation: 3/4 time has 3 quarter-note beats per measure. It is commonly used for waltzes.
